title: 64 Combat Engagements on Day 1,497; Russia Repelled at Sloviansk, Claims Podoly Near Kupyansk; 1,060 Russian Losses
detail: Ukraine's General Staff reported 64 combat clashes on April 1 (Day 1,497), bringing cumulative Russian personnel losses to approximately 1,298,730 since February 24, 2022 — with roughly 1,060 killed or wounded in the 24-hour period. Ukrainian forces repelled the largest Russian motorized assault on the Sloviansk sector since the start of 2026, with intense fighting continuing across Pokrovsk, Kostiantynivka, and Hulyaipole sectors. Russian forces claimed to have seized the village of Podoly near Kupyansk in Kharkiv Oblast; Ukrainian officials did not confirm. Russian forces made tactical advances northwest of Oleksandro-Kalynove in Donetsk Oblast and continued pressure on the Kostiantynivka area. Zaporizhzhia Oblast remained under intense Russian pressure with 975 strikes recorded across 42 settlements in a 24-hour period. Russia continued deploying guided aerial bombs and kamikaze drones along the 1,000 km frontline.

media.caption: Ukrainian soldiers repel a Russian motorized assault in the Sloviansk sector, the largest since the start of 2026

title: Trump Easter Ceasefire Deadline Conceded as Unattainable; Russia Launches Drones in Response to Zelenskyy's Offer
detail: The Trump administration conceded by April 1 that its self-imposed Easter ceasefire deadline (Orthodox Easter: April 16) was unattainable. Kremlin spokesman Dmitry Peskov had dismissed Ukraine's Easter ceasefire proposal on March 31, stating: 'We don't see any clearly articulated initiative.' Russia's response was instead a mass drone barrage across 8 Ukrainian regions. President Zelenskyy reacted publicly: 'We proposed an Easter ceasefire — in response, we receive Shaheds. We also proposed a ceasefire specifically regarding energy facilities — the Russians ignore this and again try to hit our substations and transformers.' US peace envoys Steve Witkoff and Jared Kushner continue shuttle diplomacy, with Trump and Zelenskyy reportedly 90–95% aligned on a peace framework but key territorial and security guarantee issues remain unresolved. ISW noted Russia's pattern of exploiting ceasefire discussions for operational regrouping rather than genuine de-escalation. Ukraine's Bucha Remembrance Summit (marking the 4th anniversary of the revelation of Russian war crimes) was attended by 23 foreign delegations on March 31.

media.caption: Kremlin rebuffs Zelensky's Easter ceasefire proposal as Trump deadline fails

title: Russian Drone Strike Injures 11 in Nikopol; Sloviansk Railway Hit; Civilian Toll Mounts as Russia Ignores Ceasefire Call
detail: A Russian drone strike struck Nikopol in Dnipropetrovsk Oblast on April 1, injuring 11 people including a child and damaging infrastructure. Separately, a Russian strike on railway infrastructure in Sloviansk (Donetsk Oblast) wounded 4 people, one critically. Residual fires from Russia's late March 31 multi-region barrage continued to be extinguished into April 1 in Lutsk, where drone debris damaged an apartment building, a food warehouse, and a Nova Poshta logistics terminal. A 6-year-old girl who was critically injured in a Russian strike on Sumy region on March 28 died in hospital, raising civilian toll from that attack. In Zaporizhzhia Oblast, 975 shelling incidents were recorded across 42 settlements in 24 hours. Ukraine's air defenses remained active across multiple regions overnight. Russia's sustained campaign against civilian infrastructure continued despite Zelenskyy's Easter ceasefire and energy truce proposals.

media.caption: Rescue workers at the scene of a Russian drone strike in Nikopol that injured 11 people including a child
